day-1.title: Creating Developer-Centric Content: How to Write for a Technical Audience
day-1.session_description: This session will focus on the nuances of writing for developers, including tips on simplifying complex concepts, structuring technical tutorials, and using code snippets effectively.
day-1.shortbio: Adejoke Haastrup is a seasoned software engineer with expertise in building and integrating complex systems. She is the founder of Kidsthatcode, an initiative dedicated to inspiring the next generation by encouraging kids to start coding early. Currently, she leads Developer Relations at Sendbox, where she focuses on building integrations and creating technical content that helps engineers understand the product better, while also providing comprehensive developer support.

day-1.title: Building Web Apps That Work Offline and Beyond
day-1.session_description:
In today’s always-connected world, users expect apps that work seamlessly, even when the internet doesn’t. This session will explore how you can create web apps that deliver a great experience both online and offline. We’ll dive into practical strategies for building resilient apps that keep functioning regardless of network conditions. From advanced caching techniques to leveraging cutting-edge web APIs, you’ll learn how to make your apps more reliable and user-friendly. 
Whether you’re new to offline-first development or looking to enhance your existing projects, this session will equip you with the tools and knowledge to take your web apps to the next level.
day-1.shortbio: Confidence is a Senior Developer Advocate at Cloudflare, where he plays a pivotal role in connecting developers with the company's cutting-edge technologies. With over five years of hands-on experience as a software engineer, Confidence has a deep understanding of the developer ecosystem, enabling him to effectively support enterprise clients and teams in utilizing Cloudflare's solutions for optimal success.

day-1.title: Leveling up the Kubernetes Development Experience with Remocal development
day-1.session_description:
The shift from monolithic to containerized environments was meant to improve application software development processes and ease developer experience entirely. However, it has become particularly challenging when dealing with microservices-based applications deployed in Kubernetes, with many running services.
Developer teams now have to deal with strenuous debugging cycles, frequent rebuilds, redeploys, and network overhead, which significantly slows down development cycles.

In this session, we will discuss development workflows for Kubernetes. We’ll look at the differences between traditional development, and different approaches people take to building Kubernetes services. We’ll introduce mirror and how it can be used to achieve a much faster feedback loop and optimize your Developer Experience.
day-1.shortbio: Anita is a developer advocate and a technical writer specializing in cloud native and DevOps engineering. She is championing the growth and adoption of cloud-native solutions in Africa as an organizer of Kubernetes Community Days(KCD) Nigeria and the CNCF Abuja community group. She is an Open source evangelist and has led great efforts in around DEI, Documentation, and Community growth as a member of CHAOSS Board of directors.

day-1.title: Building a Multi-Tenant SaaS with Microservices on Kubernetes
day-1.session_description: In today’s digital landscape, APIs are integral to application functionality, but they also present significant security challenges. This session will delve into effective strategies for building resilient APIs that can withstand and mitigate various types of attacks. We will cover essential techniques for designing APIs with built-in defenses.
day-1.shortbio: Micheal is an Information Security Engineer at a leading provider of innovative, data-driven, end-to-end technology platforms in Africa. He also works as a Penetration Tester, applying his skills and knowledge in ethical hacking, vulnerability assessment, network security, and web development.

day-2.title: Gamification in UX: Creating Engaging User Experiences Through Play
day-2.session_description:
Gamification is transforming the way we design user experiences by integrating game mechanics into non-gaming environments. 
This session will explore the principles of gamification in UX design, illustrating how these techniques can be used to create more engaging and enjoyable user experiences. Attendees will learn practical strategies for incorporating gamification into their projects to enhance user interaction and satisfaction
day-2.shortbio: Ololade Adesuyi is a seasoned UI/UX Designer with over 4 years of hands-on experience in creating user-centric digital experiences. Currently a Product Design Lead at Tappi, Ololade has a rich background in product design, usability, and digital transformation. She has contributed significantly to the tech community, volunteering as a Design Mentor and speaking at various tech events and conferences. Her expertise lies in designing intuitive, engaging user interfaces that not only meet but exceed user expectations.

day-2.title: The Lazy Person's Guide to Productivity 2.0
day-2.session_description: As a follow up to my workshop at DevFest Lagos 2023, I'd be doing a deep dive into how to be excel as a lazy person. If you're looking for a more detailed description, then looks like you aren't lazy.
day-2.shortbio: Sodiq Akinjobi is a seasoned Program Manager with over 5 years of experience in global tech. He has a background in engineering and is currently pursuing an MBA. His expertise lies in community management, developer relations, and program scaling. He's currently at Google, where he's powering developer growth in Africa through community engagement, education, and resource allocation.

day-2.title: Decentralization is Not Open Source: Unraveling the Misconceptions
day-2.session_description:
In the rapidly evolving landscape of technology, two buzzwords have dominated conversations: "decentralization" and "open source". While often mentioned in the same breath and perceived as interchangeable, they represent fundamentally different concepts with distinct implications for the future of digital infrastructure. 
This talk aims to clarify the misconceptions surrounding decentralization and open source, exploring how a project can be decentralized but not necessarily open source, and vice versa.
day-2.shortbio: Samson Goddy is a seasoned open source program manager and DevRel professional with over a decade of experience working with multicultural teams, excelling in community building, advocacy, and strategic partnerships within developer communities. Co-founder of Open Source Community Africa (OSCA), and is currently involved in open source work with Hedera Hashgraph, focusing on community growth around its hashgraph consensus algorithm. Samson is also a GitHub Star and serves on the board of the Open Source Collective.

day-2.title: Implementing OAuth 2.0 for Secure API Access
day-2.session_description:
In this hands-on session, you'll learn to implement OAuth 2.0 to secure your API endpoints. We'll cover:

- OAuth 2.0 fundamentals and flow types
- Setting up an authorization server
- Implementing client-side authentication
- Securing API endpoints with access tokens
- Token validation and refresh mechanisms
- Best practices and common pitfalls

You'll build a working OAuth 2.0 system, integrating it with a sample API.
By the end, you'll have practical experience implementing robust API security using industry-standard protocols.

Prerequisites: Basic knowledge of web development and API concepts.
day-2.shortbio: Motunrayo Koyejo is an accomplished backend engineer with over 4 years of experience in crafting secure and scalable financial applications. Known for her innovative approach to solving complex challenges, she brings a practical and results-oriented perspective to every project. Beyond her technical work, Motunrayo is a dedicated mentor, guiding the next generation of engineers through volunteer initiatives.
